title: Houses for Rent in Tremont
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Tremont, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Tremont, Cleveland? See rent ranges near Lincoln Park, transit options, and voucher-friendly homes. Book a free showing.
answerBox: Tremont is a near-west-side Cleveland neighborhood built around Lincoln Park, in ZIP code 44113. Rental houses here are a mix of single-family homes and duplexes, and Rent Finder Cleveland manages a number of voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Tremont for renters using Housing Choice Vouchers.

body: Tremont is a near-west-side Cleveland neighborhood centered on Lincoln Park, known locally as an arts and dining district. It sits in **ZIP code 44113**, just across the Cuyahoga Valley from Downtown and adjacent to Ohio City and Clark-Fulton. Rental housing stock in Tremont is a mix of single-family homes and duplexes, consistent with the broader Cleveland rental market.

Rent Finder Cleveland manages a number of voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Tremont. Every home in our portfolio accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or tell us what you're looking for and we'll follow up.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house in Tremont?
body: Cleveland-wide market data offers a useful benchmark for Tremont renters. Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, Cleveland's median asking rent is **$1,250/month** overall (up 4.2% year-over-year), with 2-bedroom homes at **$1,100** and 3-bedroom homes at **$1,350**. RentCafe (Yardi), reporting July 2, 2026, shows a citywide average of **$1,564/month** across professionally managed apartments — a figure that skews higher because it weights larger managed complexes rather than standalone rental houses.

Within Rent Finder Cleveland's own portfolio of 90+ Section 8-ready homes across Greater Cleveland, rents typically range from **$700 to $1,800 a month**, with most homes landing around **$1,000**. Two- and three-bedroom homes make up the largest share of what we manage.

Tremont borders Ohio City to the north and Clark-Fulton, home to the MetroHealth main campus, to the west. The neighborhood is served by RTA bus routes connecting to Downtown Cleveland and nearby near-west-side neighborhoods, and it sits close to I-490 and I-71 for drivers heading toward Downtown or the southern suburbs.

items: **Landmark:** Lincoln Park, the neighborhood's central green space and arts/dining hub
**Transit:** RTA bus routes to Downtown; near I-490 and I-71
**Nearby neighborhoods:** Ohio City, Clark-Fulton (MetroHealth corridor), Downtown

sections.h2: Section 8 and housing vouchers in Tremont
body: Renters using a Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) apply through the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)**, which runs the program for Cuyahoga County. CMHA accepts preliminary applications year-round via its [online voucher portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), and applicants are selected through periodic mini-lotteries rather than a strict waiting-list order.

It's worth being clear-eyed here: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland — Tremont included — has not passed a local source-of-income ordinance as of this writing (a small set of Cuyahoga County suburbs, like Cleveland Heights, have their own protections). Independent of what local law requires, every Rent Finder Cleveland home, including those in and around Tremont, welcomes Section 8 vouchers and is kept HUD-inspection-ready.

Tremont's arts-and-dining reputation and near-west-side location draw renters who want proximity to Downtown Cleveland without living directly in it. Renters comparing neighborhoods should also see our guide to [houses for rent in Ohio City](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/), just to the north across the Cuyahoga Valley, or our broader [Cleveland houses for rent pillar page](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for citywide pricing context.

Whether you're a first-time renter, relocating for work near MetroHealth or Downtown, or applying with a Housing Choice Voucher, the most reliable way to see current availability near Tremont is to reach out directly, since third-party listing sites can lag behind what's actually on the market.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Tremont in?
faq.a: Tremont is in ZIP code 44113, on Cleveland's near-west side, built around Lincoln Park and bordered by Ohio City to the north and Clark-Fulton to the west.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Tremont?
faq.a: Yes. Rent Finder Cleveland manages a number of voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Tremont, and every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a free showing to check current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Tremont?
faq.a: Citywide Cleveland data (July 2026) puts 2-bedroom rents between roughly $1,100 and $1,818 a month and 3-bedroom rents between about $1,350 and $2,716, depending on the source. Rent Finder Cleveland's own homes across Greater Cleveland typically rent from $700 to $1,800, with most around $1,000.

faq.q: Is Section 8 protected by law in Tremont and the rest of Cleveland?
faq.a: No statewide Ohio law requires landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland has not enacted its own source-of-income ordinance as of mid-2026. A handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs, including Cleveland Heights, do have local protections. Renters should confirm current rules before assuming any landlord must accept a voucher.

faq.q: What's near Tremont for renters without a car?
faq.a: Tremont is served by RTA bus routes connecting to Downtown Cleveland and neighboring near-west-side areas, and it's within a short drive of the RTA Red Line's Ohio City rapid-transit station just across the neighborhood line.
